sections.paragraph:
ἀναστρέφω [ἀνά, στρέφω]

—1. ‘cause to be in total disarray by turning upside down’, upset, overturn J 2:15 v.l.

—2. ‘retrace one’s movement to a point’, go back, return Ac 5:22; 15:16 (in a conflated LXX citation, where the verb ἀ. expresses the idea that after apparent delay God ‘returns’ with mercy).

—3. pass. in act. sense ‘go about in a locality’, stay, live Mt 17:22 v.l.

—4. pass. in act. sense ‘conduct/behave oneself’, either in an appropriate or inappropriate manner (common in honorary ins.), conduct oneself, live 2 Cor 1:12; Eph 2:3; 1 Ti 3:15; Hb 10:33; 13:18; 1 Pt 1:17; 2 Pt 2:18 (here in imagery of teachers who ‘wander about’ in ‘deceptive error’).
